reasoning_model_1: Model 1 demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy. It correctly identified the text to be spoken ('嘿！小王！这边！我跟你说个秘密，老师的课真的好无聊。') from the user's prompt, excluding the instructional part ('请先用大声开始...'). By isolating the target dialogue, it sets a strong foundation for the paralinguistic performance. Assuming the audio features align with the text structure—where '嘿！小王！这边！' (0.0s - 2.0s) serves as the loud, attention-grabbing segment, and '我跟你说个秘密...' (2.0s - end) serves as the secretive, quiet segment—the model successfully executes the single-round generation task without reading the instructions or adding extraneous content.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails significantly in Content Accuracy. Instead of simply speaking the target text with the required volume changes, the model generated a script that includes stage directions and hallucinated content not present in the prompt. Specifically, it spoke descriptive phrases like '大声的喊出来' (Shout out loud) and '然后突然压低声音轻声说' (Then suddenly lower voice to say), and added unrequested dialogue ('歪，老师今天又讲了一堆没意思的东西'). This explicitly violates the requirement to read the specific sentence provided. The inclusion of meta-instructions and extra text changes the meaning and purpose of the audio completely, rendering it unusable for the intended command.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. The evaluation criterion primarily demands Content Accuracy and Paralinguistic Compliance. Model 1 correctly extracted and spoke only the target text, which allows the paralinguistic features (volume change) to be applied effectively to the intended content. Model 2, conversely, failed at the most basic level of Content Accuracy. It suffered from severe instruction-following issues, reading out the style descriptions ('Shout out loud') and hallucinating a new scenario rather than performing the requested speech act. According to the scoring standards, if the content is clearly inconsistent or completely changed (as seen in Model 2's inclusion of meta-text and extra dialogue), it must receive a score of 0.
